_note: 100 short financial text snippets for quantization-recall testing. Mix of earnings, news, regulatory.
docs: Apple reported $89.5B in Q3 2025 revenue, beating consensus by $1.2B.
Tim Cook said iPhone demand in China was softer than expected.
Services revenue grew 16 percent year over year to $24.2 billion.
Apple authorized a $90B share buyback program over the next two years.
Vision Pro shipments were below initial projections due to inventory cuts.
Microsoft Azure revenue grew 29 percent constant currency last quarter.
Satya Nadella said AI workloads now drive over 7 percent of Azure consumption.
Microsoft Cloud gross margin improved 200 basis points to 73 percent.
Microsoft's effective tax rate dropped to 18 percent after the Ireland ruling.
Activision Blizzard contributed $2.1B in revenue post-merger.
Berkshire Hathaway trimmed its Apple stake by 49 percent in Q2 2024.
Warren Buffett said taxes on gains influenced the decision to sell.
Berkshire's cash pile grew to $325 billion at quarter end.
Apple remains Berkshire's largest equity holding by market value.
Greg Abel discussed Berkshire's energy investments in detail.
Tesla deliveries fell 8 percent quarter over quarter in Q3.
Elon Musk forecast Cybertruck volume above 250000 units in 2026.
Tesla's gross margin compressed to 17.9 percent excluding regulatory credits.
Energy storage deployments hit a record 6.9 GWh in the quarter.
FSD subscription growth accelerated to 22 percent month over month.
Nvidia Q3 data center revenue reached $30.8B, up 112 percent year over year.
Jensen Huang said Blackwell ramp is on schedule for production this quarter.
Nvidia gross margin held above 75 percent on data center mix.
Customer concentration is a stated risk in Nvidia's 10-Q filing.
Nvidia networking revenue (Infiniband + Spectrum-X) reached $3.1B.
Meta Reality Labs operating loss widened to $4.4B for the quarter.
Meta's AI infrastructure capex guidance raised to $40 billion for 2025.
Family of Apps revenue grew 19 percent year over year.
Meta repurchased $9.5B of its own stock during the quarter.
Mark Zuckerberg said Threads now has over 275 million monthly active users.
Amazon AWS revenue reached $27.4B, up 19 percent year over year.
Andy Jassy said AI workloads in AWS are a multi-billion run-rate business.
Amazon's retail operating margin expanded to 6.1 percent.
Capex guidance for 2025 is approximately $75 billion, weighted to AI.
International segment swung to a $1.3B operating profit.
Google Cloud revenue grew 35 percent to $11.4B.
Sundar Pichai said Gemini usage is up 4x year over year on free tier.
Search advertising revenue grew 12 percent excluding currency.
YouTube ad revenue beat consensus at $8.9B for the quarter.
Pixel sales reached an all-time high in Q3 2025.
JPMorgan reported $13.2B in net income, beating consensus.
Jamie Dimon warned about geopolitical risks impacting global trade.
Net interest income exceeded $23B on resilient deposit balances.
Credit card charge-offs ticked up to 3.6 percent annualized.
Investment banking revenue rose 31 percent year over year.
Federal Reserve held the federal funds rate steady at 4.50 to 4.75 percent.
Powell signaled the FOMC will be patient on further rate cuts.
The dot plot now shows two cuts expected over the next twelve months.
Core PCE inflation came in at 2.7 percent year over year.
Treasury yields fell modestly after the FOMC statement.
Goldman Sachs reported Q3 earnings of $8.40 per share, beating estimates.
Trading revenue surged 32 percent on fixed income volatility.
Investment banking backlog hit a record level at quarter end.
David Solomon noted strong M&A pipeline activity.
Goldman raised its 2025 S&P 500 target to 6500.
Bank of America posted $25.5B in net interest income for the quarter.
Consumer banking deposit balances stabilized at $963B.
Brian Moynihan said credit quality remained benign across all segments.
Wealth management AUM crossed $4 trillion for the first time.
Bank of America announced a 4 percent dividend hike.
Wells Fargo earnings exceeded expectations on lower provisions.
Charlie Scharf said the asset cap will remain in place into 2025.
Auto loan originations declined 12 percent year over year.
Wells Fargo repurchased $3.5B of common stock during the quarter.
Net interest margin compressed by 6 basis points sequentially.
Costco same-store sales grew 7.2 percent excluding fuel and currency.
E-commerce sales jumped 19 percent at Costco for the most recent month.
Costco's executive membership renewal rate hit 92.7 percent.
Membership fee increase contributed approximately $40M to operating income.
Costco signaled possible international expansion in Vietnam next year.
Walmart raised its full-year guidance on stronger grocery traffic.
Sam's Club comparable sales grew 6.4 percent excluding fuel.
Walmart+ subscriber growth remains in the double digits.
Walmart's advertising business grew 28 percent year over year.
International segment delivered 12 percent constant-currency growth.
Home Depot warned of soft demand from professional contractors.
Same-store sales fell 1.3 percent on weather and rate-sensitivity.
Home Depot acquired SRS Distribution for $18.25B earlier in the year.
Big-ticket discretionary purchases declined more than mid-singles.
Lowe's reaffirmed its prior full-year same-store sales guidance.
Pfizer reported Comirnaty sales of $1.4B for the quarter, beating estimates.
Albert Bourla said the obesity pipeline remains a strategic priority.
Paxlovid sales were essentially zero in non-US markets in Q3.
Pfizer plans $1.5B in additional cost reductions by end of 2025.
Eli Lilly's Mounjaro sales reached $3.1B in the quarter, beating consensus.
Zepbound sales hit $1.7B in its first full quarter at scale.
David Ricks said supply is now meeting demand for tirzepatide products.
Eli Lilly raised full-year EPS guidance by approximately 10 percent.
Novo Nordisk Wegovy supply constraints are expected to ease in Q1 2026.
JPMorgan downgraded Boeing on continued production quality concerns.
Boeing Max 737 deliveries reached 23 per month, below the FAA-allowed cap.
Boeing reported a $2.4B Q3 operating loss on defense charges.
Free cash flow burn slowed to $1.3B from $4.3B in the prior quarter.
United Technologies disclosed a new $1.2B charge related to engine recalls.
Lockheed Martin reported a $1.6B backlog increase from Sentinel contract.
F-35 deliveries resumed after the TR-3 software certification cleared.
Lockheed announced a $7B share buyback authorization.
Raytheon's Patriot order book swelled on European demand.
Northrop Grumman's space segment grew 17 percent on Sentinel ramp.
Disney's parks segment operating income rose 6 percent on attendance gains.
Disney+ subscribers crossed 156 million paid globally.
Bob Iger said the streaming business will hit consistent profitability in fiscal 2025.
ESPN flagship streaming service launches in Q3 calendar 2026.
Hulu Live TV pricing was raised 8 percent for new subscribers.
